title: weinmannia microphylla var. microphylla
intro: Weinmannia microphylla var. microphylla, also known as the Little Raoul or Small Tukau, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae, native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Weinmannia microphylla var. microphylla include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic, making it a useful remedy for skin conditions and injuries. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health benefits.
